title: Torque Signal Circuit Check

figures_detected.description: Schematic showing ECCS C/U terminal 25 connected to N-CVT C/U terminal 13
Data monitor display showing range switch statuses and TORQUE SIG ON, with RECORD button. Accelerator pedal and brake pedal operation illustration for section A.
N-CVT C/U connector diagram showing voltmeter connected to terminal 13 and body ground for voltage check. Accelerator pedal and brake pedal operation illustration for section A.

content_blocks.title: ECCS C/U to N-CVT C/U connection

nodes.text:
A — Check input signal
1. Apply parking brake. Set selector lever to "P".
Start engine.
Press brake pedal.
Select "D".
2. Select "DATA MONITOR"
• Check that "TORQUE SIGNAL" display is "ON" when accelerator pedal is released. Check that "OFF" blinks at Stall speed (1,700-2,300 rpm) after brake pedal is fully depressed and accelerator pedal is depressed.
NOTE: Refer to "Diagnostic procedure", "Stall test".
— OR —
• Connect tester between N-CVT control unit terminal #13 and body ground. Check that voltage is 0V when accelerator pedal is released. Check that voltage is 12V at stall speed (1,700-2,300 rpm) after brake pedal is fully depressed and accelerator pedal is depressed.
NOTE: Refer to "Diagnostic procedure", "Stall test"
Perform self diagnosis after test drive

Check following items:
1. Continuity of harness between E.C.C.S. control unit and N-CVT control unit
2. E.C.C.S. control unit output voltage (refer to section EF&EC)
3. Throttle sensor (refer to section EF&EC)
4. Accelerator position switch installation
5. Faulty shifting (refer to "Diagnostic Procedures", "Faulty Shifting").

1. Check N-CVT control unit I/O signals
2. Recheck N-CVT control unit pin terminals for damage or incorrect connection of control unit harness connector

model_notes: Applies to N-CVT (automatic transmission) equipped vehicles.
